architecture_plan.title: Fast End-to-End Performance Simulation Architecture for Accelerated Hardware-Software Stacks
architecture_plan.overview: This architecture implements a minimalist full-stack simulation framework inspired by NEX and DSim, designed to achieve orders-of-magnitude faster performance simulation while maintaining accuracy. The system separates native execution components from simulated ones, with precise synchronization mechanisms to ensure correctness. The core idea is to simulate only unavailable components and run the rest natively, with cycle-accurate simulation of performance-critical aspects of simulated components.

modules.purpose: Manages the overall simulation workflow, including synchronization, runtime execution, scheduling, and time warping for seamless integration of native and simulated components.
Handles the precise synchronization between natively executing components and simulated components, ensuring correct timing and data flow across the hybrid execution environment.
Provides the runtime environment for executing native components and managing the interface with simulated components, including memory management and component lifecycle control.
Determines which components to simulate versus execute natively, based on availability and performance-criticality, and manages the scheduling of simulation tasks.
Simulates performance-critical aspects of unavailable components with cycle accuracy, computing both performance and functionality while maintaining synchronization with native execution.
Computes performance using Labelled Petri Nets (LPNs), enabling accurate modeling of complex system behaviors and timing relationships in simulated components.
Handles the functional simulation of components, ensuring that simulated behavior matches the expected operational characteristics of the hardware/software being modeled.
Ensures that performance and functionality simulation are properly synchronized with native execution, maintaining consistency across all components.
Provides modular composition interfaces between native and simulated components, enabling flexible integration and configuration of different system stacks.

framework_suggestions: PyPy or CPython with Cython for performance-critical native execution
Event-driven simulation framework like SimPy for managing simulation events
Petri net libraries such as PyPES or custom LPN implementation for performance modeling
Multi-threading or async frameworks like asyncio for concurrent execution of native and simulated components
Mocking and testing frameworks like pytest for validating simulation accuracy and performance
